decision: Defer implementing AI-coding PTY persistence (task-002) until host-mode terminals work; node-pty native spawn fails (posix_spawnp) in this checkout so the terminal-core change cannot be verified end-to-end.
rationale: Root cause is code-verified (PTY lifetime==WS lifetime, ephemeral client session list, no reattach: connection-handler.ts:66/175/329-383, TerminalManager.tsx:198). Shipping a blind change to the critical terminal path violates the no-guess / run-tests guardrails. Operator approved deferral.
alternatives: Implement server logic with mocked-pty unit tests now (rejected by operator: integration risk without e2e); investigate node-pty build first (deferred to its own task).

decision: Approved PTY-persistence design (to implement once unblocked): on WS close keep the PTY/container alive (detached) keyed by a stable clientSessionId; server ring buffer of recent output; reconnecting WS reattaches by clientSessionId and replays the buffer then streams live; client persists session list in localStorage and reconnects on mount; explicit stop kills PTY+container; GC a detached session when no WS reconnects within a grace window (no separate heartbeat channel).
rationale: Operator selected reattach+scrollback-replay and browser-lifetime retention; advisor recommended grace-window GC over a heartbeat to avoid sleep/background-tab/network-drop false GC.
alternatives: Live-only reattach (less faithful); fixed short GC window (loses long-absent agents); heartbeat-based GC (fragile edge cases).
